blocks.content: An hour out, the conversation found its footing the way their conversations always did — not from a starting point but from the middle, as though it had been quietly running in both of them and they'd just decided to say it out loud.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: What did you think? About today. All of it.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I think today was the best day of my life. And I'm aware that's something people say, but I mean it in a way I didn't expect to.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: That's still not very specific.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I don't have vocabulary for it yet. Give me a few years.
She smiled at the road ahead.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: The vows.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: When you were saying them — the part about sickness and health.
He was quiet for a moment. Not uncomfortable — the quiet of someone approaching something he'd thought about carefully and wanted to say right.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Cass, I meant every word of it equally. I meant the whole thing. There was no part where I was saying it just because the ceremony required it.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I know. I just — I noticed you didn't hesitate on that part.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Why would I ever hesitate on that? I've known what I was saying yes to since the Ferris wheel. All of it. Every part. I didn't need the vows to make that true — the vows just let me say it in front of everyone who matters to you.
She looked at him. He kept his eyes on the road, but there was something in his expression that was entirely open — not performing steadiness, just being it.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I cried through all of mine.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I know. It was—
A small pause.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Don't say beautiful.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I was going to say important. That you weren't performing it. That every word came from somewhere real. I could see it and it meant everything to me.
She looked at him for a moment longer than she usually allowed herself.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You were the only person in that room who could tell the difference.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: That's not true. Lena could.
She laughed — sudden, real, the kind that surprised her.
The road opened wider ahead of them. Darkness and direction and his hand finding hers across the console.
